cpu: AMD A8-7650K Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: Some gliches during gameplay. Need shadow fix.
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®

notes:
How it looks OOTB: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H3QOFfdIWiE
"SplinterCell.ini"
"ForceShadowMode=0"
and it works
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®

notes: Used the shadow tweak and have to hide the panel of Xfce because it showed up during game was running. Despite of that, the game is running fine.
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®

notes: Game runs out of the box. Played it 9 hours and there wasn't any problem. Not need tweak. Runs as native.
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®

notes: The game works flawlessly after applying the Widescreen Fix by ThirteenAG ( https://github.com/ThirteenAG/WidescreenFixesPack/releases/tag/sc ) and d3d8to9 by crosire ( https://github.com/crosire/d3d8to9/releases ). Cutscenes, though, seem not to work, only playing audio and nothing else but a black screen. Removing "Logos.bik" and "videointro.bik" allows the game to instantly jump into the main menu, allowing to launch the game without having to wait 15 seconds beforehand.
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®

notes: Works great. Only real issue is you might need to enable projector shadows in SplinterCell.ini as the default buffer shadows are broken. That's also a problem on Windows though, so I can't fault Proton/Wine for that.
title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell®
